items.speaker: Academy Instructor
items.text: The Academy has reviewed the incident, Professor Warren. While we share your concern over the Institute's loss, blaming a single student...

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: What were you doing when Hyvatia lost control, Miss Lynae? Why were you at the Institute during class hours?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I was escorting {PlayerName} to burn {Male=his;Female=her} Cassette at the Institute.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: On my way back to the Academy, I noticed abnormal frequency spikes and contaminated Exoswarm near Sundermere.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Before I could act, I was surrounded. I tried to contact the Academy, but the Void Storm blocked communications.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Exoswarm remains should still be there. I assume... you are already investigating them?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: No such remains were found, Miss Lynae. Making things up won't help you.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That was quick.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: What about the surveillance cameras? They're equipped to handle Void Storm interference. They should've captured the Exoswarm before things escalated.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: They didn't. And in fact, you aren't in the footage at all. I wonder what you were doing at that time. Avoiding the cameras, perhaps?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What if the footage was tampered with? Her presence could've been erased.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Possible. But only if she and the Exoswarm were truly there.

items.speaker: Academy Instructor
items.text: Since neither side can confirm that, how about we start by verifying if the footage was altered?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: We should expand the scope of the investigation. Remember the security breach in the database? There might be more compromised than just today's feed.

items.speaker: Academy Instructor
items.text: You mean the breach that exposed a flaw in the system? Fair enough.

items.speaker: Ms. Voss
items.text: Do you have anything else to add, Miss Lynae? Don't worry, we're here to help you.

items.speaker: Academy Instructor
items.text: Then please remain on Academy grounds until the investigation is concluded.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: One last thing, Miss Lynae. You're from the New Federation, right?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: What do you mean?

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Nothing. I just noticed you stay at the Academy even during breaks. Never thought of going home?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Thanks for asking, Professor. I keep in touch with my parents whenever I can. If you're implying I have some secret agenda, you'll be disappointed.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Not at all. I simply wonder if you don't want to return.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I don't think that's something you should concern yourself with, Professor.

items.speaker: Ms. Voss
items.text: Let's not waste any more time, Professor Warren.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Phew...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Thanks… for speaking up for me.

options.text: Why not tell them the truth?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What was that device you had when Hyvatia went berserk?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Straight to the point, huh? I thought you'd let it slide, like they did.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Highly unlikely.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Good... you're still you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What's your real purpose?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Let's talk somewhere else. This place is suffocating.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: ({Male=He;Female=She} should've made it to the Institute by now. No danger there... I'll handle this without dragging {Male=him;Female=her} into it.)

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: (That message I just got... It's from the Fractsidus "liaison.")

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: (The Void Storm should've erased him… Yet here I am getting a message from a "dead" man.)

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: (Someone's using his comms ID. They know what I did, but haven't blown my cover. A threat? Or something else?)

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: (Last signal pinged from around here.)

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: (Contaminated <te href=700022>Exoswarm</te>?)

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: (Better clear them out first.)

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: You got here quicker than I expected. No hurry. We've got time before the main event.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: You...!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Impossible. Who are you really?

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: The poor Fractsidus liaison you... "handled"?

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: Or… just an innocent researcher?

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: It's all up to you, Lynae—who you choose to be, and what you're willing to lose.

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: That look… Still unsure, I see. I could help you out a little.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Spare me. Whatever you're planning, I'll stop it.

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: Oh? So the "Nameless Fugitive" is now the "Unseen Guardian"?

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: Turn me in, and the Spacetrek Collective will come for you, too. Tell me, Lynae, are you really as resolute as you think?

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: Either way, you're a problem for me. So here's a parting gift... one fitting for your new role.

items.speaker: "Liaison"
items.text: The same gift you've always received—"doubt" and "isolation."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Those were… my memories? But the last one…)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Something wrong? Feeling unwell?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: ...No, I'm fine. So, this is a <te href=700006>Rabelle Cassette</te>?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes. Your frequency has been copied and transcribed into it.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But before it can be put into service, it needs calibration. Would you hand it over for a moment?

items.speaker: Burning Room Researcher
items.text: Hm? So that noise earlier wasn't an error. This frequency code… I've seen the exact same one in the database.

items.speaker: Burning Room Researcher
items.text: Identical codes caused a conflict during transcribing. Normally, Resonators from similar origins have close matches. But a perfect match? That's almost unheard of.

items.speaker: Burning Room Researcher
items.text: And stranger still, it's not in any official archive. The file is… empty.

options.text: Are you sure nothing else is in there?

items.speaker: Burning Room Researcher
items.text: Just some unreadable data. Not corruption. More like… someone deleted it.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Could it have been me who left it behind? But if I deleted it myself… why leave any trace?)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Just a data error. Overwriting should solve it.

items.speaker: Burning Room Researcher
items.text: Ah, understood, Professor Mornye.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: This <te href=700007>Frequency Cassette Recorder</te>, or FCR, will alert you when a Void Storm appears. It also dampens the effects of the Storm and stabilizes your existence here in Lahai-Roi.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: So keep it on you at all times.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But without adequate precautions, it still won't fully protect you from the Void Storm's effects. Many surveyors and students have had their frequencies erased this way.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Professor Mornye, about my frequency...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: …Mm, I have read your report. You're stable for now. Compared to your enrollment check-up, your Rabelle's Curve has leveled out. But yes, your frequency still shows signs of past loss.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: As for the Voidworm, there's no precedent... No one's ever survived having their frequency devoured. Whether yours can be restored is unknown.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: So don't take unnecessary risks. That's rule number one.

options.text: I came here to find out what happened to my frequency.
I don't know much about this place yet.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I understand. I'll keep watch for anything that might help.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Ask me anything you need to know.

items.speaker: Flustered Assistant
items.text: Professor Mornye! What a relief to see you—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Actually—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Well then...
I appreciate your perspective, Professor Anslem.
But according to Royan calculations, this launch window is the best we've had in decades.

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: How can you be so certain, Warren? Their "optimal window" is derived from those indecipherable runes, with no methodology disclosed. Even our most advanced computational systems have failed to decipher their process!

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: The Roya are our partners, Professor.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Their calculations are unquestionable. All we can do is uphold our side of the bargain and make sure the New Solar Ceremony goes off without a hitch.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: Well then... I believe counsel from a third person may serve us well at this point.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I'm inclined to proceed with the original plan.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But given the unsatisfying results from the last simulation, I understand Professor Anslem's concerns. I'll go to Bjartr Woods to consult with the Heliodic Six and reverify the parameters.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: What matters now is ensuring that the Institute can calculate and calibrate the Reactor Drive's data in time for the launch of the new sun.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: We all know the importance of <te href=700013>Hyvatia</te> in this process, and I sincerely hope you'll place more trust in it, Anslem.

items.speaker: Warren
items.text: This is a shared achievement between the Collective and the Roya, the very future of Lahai-Roi. I respect your caution and the pride you take in your work. But please, don't let past failures hold you back now.

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Hmph, fine words… Just don't forget that I warned you.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Now that you're caught up, may I ask you a question?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Would you like to attend the New Solar Ceremony?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Me? But... I'm just a freshman.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Consider this my personal invitation.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I… I'd want you there to witness it. The rise of the new sun, and humanity taking their first steps into the future they've built with their own hands.

options.text: Since you put it that way...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'd be honored to witness the rise of Lahai-Roi's new sun.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Then it's settled.

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: Void Storm detected near Sundermere... Readings rising rapidly!

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: The source is... the Hyvatia Detector?!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Give me a full report.

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: Yes, Professor. Hyvatia's been compromised by the Void Storm. It's gone rogue. The Defense Array is activating...

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: Their target is... the Institute itself.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The Void Storm has corrupted the Hyvatia Detector, a critical sensor for the Reactor Drive. It's off course and now targeting us.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: A surface detector built by the Spacetrek Collective for the Reactor Drive's inspection and maintenance. It's vital for the Ceremony's data calibration. And now, at the worst possible time…

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: Wait, someone's near the Detector! Is that… a student?

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: What?! I never authorized this command… Ugh, I can't stop it!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What's the situation?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hyvatia's contamination triggered the highest-level safety protocol. We'll all be sealed in here until the threat is neutralized.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I tried to stop it… but my access has been denied.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Then we need to find a way to get out there.

items.speaker: Lab Researcher
items.text: Wait, the main train's suspended under lockdown protocol!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Professor Mornye, I need to borrow an Institute vehicle.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: We have backup vehicles at the transfer gate. But how do you plan to reach it?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The same way I got in!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Watch out! You have to go!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: The weather's really nice today.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: How do you feel? Settling in alright?

options.text: Not bad. The wind feels nice.
You flirted with the speed limit the entire way...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Yeah. Not bad, huh?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Heh, I'll take that as a compliment on my driving skills…

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: But honestly, it's been ages since I last raced with someone else. Getting to take you for a ride like this? Makes my day!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You like racing that much?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Uh-huh. The roar of the engine, wind in my face... It's like I've got my destination and I'm the one calling the shots.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Look... Lahai-Roi's sky might be artificial, but I found more freedom under it than I've ever known anywhere else.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Yeah. I mean, you've only got one life, right? Might as well live it the way you want. When to brake, hit the gas. Hell, even the paint job of your ride. Doesn't matter if you're a normie or a Resonator, everyone should have that choice.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That's what Startorch Academy is to me. Before I came here, I never imagined a place like this could exist. Full of people so pure, so driven...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: With the resolve to burn in the search for answers. With the belief they can actually end the Void Storm and the Threnodian... and maybe break through the Etheric Sea to find what true freedom really looks like.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: It's beautiful, isn't it?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Watching them chase their dreams… It's hard not to want that for yourself.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: But you're already a student here. You have what it takes to chase your dreams too.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Yeah, you're right.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: …But I've gotta say, things are plenty good enough right now.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ah, almost forgot—

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Welcome! You're now officially a Startorch Academy student!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: My first introduction was too rushed, so I'm doing it properly this time. I'm Lynae, a second-year in the Preparatory Program.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Time flies. Feels like I just got here, and now I'm a second-year. Hehe, you could call me Miss Lynae, if you want.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Well then, pleased to meet you... Miss Lynae?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Maybe just stick with Lynae, haha. Not really used to the whole formality thing.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: All right, let's get moving! Time to hit the road!
